value.body:
## Summary

Support native-feeling macOS two-finger horizontal trackpad gestures in the Electron desktop client so users can swipe to navigate backward and forward through Cycle routes, similar to Chrome history navigation.

## Scope

- Target `@cycle/desktop` on macOS (`window.cycleDesktop.platform === "darwin"`).
- Add back and forward navigation gestures for the main Cycle workspace shell.
- Align gesture navigation with existing React Router hash-route behavior and the current `navigateBack` fallback in `src/renderer/screens/WorkspaceScreen.tsx`.
- Preserve existing keyboard navigation, including `Escape` for back and `g ...` workspace shortcuts.
- Do not change behavior on Windows or Linux.

## Current State

- Desktop is Electron-based (`electron` dependency currently declared in `packages/desktop/package.json`).
- `WorkspaceScreen` owns workspace navigation with `useNavigate`, `navigateWorkspace`, `routeHistoryRef`, and the existing `navigateBack` callback.
- Routes are hash-based via `createHashRouter` in `src/renderer/Router.tsx`.
- The preload bridge already exposes `platform`, so renderer code can gate macOS-only behavior without expanding native APIs.
- Electron `BrowserWindow` is configured in `src/main/DesktopWindowLive.ts` through `secureWebPreferences` in `src/platform/BrowserWindows.ts`.

## Goals

- A two-finger swipe in the backward direction navigates to the previous Cycle route when available.
- A two-finger swipe in the forward direction navigates forward after a prior back navigation when browser/app history allows it.
- A single physical swipe produces at most one navigation.
- Gesture detection should feel intentional: horizontal-dominant, thresholded, and debounced.
- Normal vertical scrolling and horizontal scrolling inside scrollable UI regions must continue to work.

## Non-Goals

- Do not add a visible navigation UI.
- Do not implement a custom animated Chrome-style page transition in this pass.
- Do not add gesture support for Windows precision touchpads until macOS behavior is proven.
- Do not rely on undocumented native Electron events unless a spike proves DOM wheel events are insufficient.

## Plan

1. Extract workspace history actions from `WorkspaceScreen` into named callbacks:
   - `navigateBackFromHistory()` keeps the current stack behavior and parent-route fallback.
   - `navigateForwardFromHistory()` calls `navigate(1)` only when forward history is expected/available, or remains a no-op if we cannot determine availability safely.
   - Keep route persistence through `writeStoredWorkspacePath` unchanged.

2. Add a small renderer hook, for example `useMacTrackpadSwipeNavigation`, mounted from `WorkspaceScreen`:
   - Enable only when `getDesktopBridge()?.platform === "darwin"`.
   - Listen for `wheel` events at the app shell/window level with capture enabled.
   - Treat a gesture as navigation only when `abs(deltaX)` materially exceeds `abs(deltaY)` and an accumulated horizontal threshold is crossed.
   - Normalize `deltaMode` values before thresholding.
   - Apply a cooldown, roughly 500-800ms, so inertial scrolling does not trigger repeated navigation.
   - Call `event.preventDefault()` only once a navigation gesture is accepted.

3. Add guardrails before navigating:
   - Ignore gestures when onboarding/setup/create-issue flows disable navigation shortcuts.
   - Ignore events with modifier keys pressed.
   - Ignore events originating from editable controls, textareas, selects, contenteditable regions, or active dialogs/forms.
   - Ignore gestures inside elements that can still scroll horizontally in the gesture direction.
   - Prefer existing app navigation state over raw browser history where possible.

4. Consider native-feel Electron settings after manual validation:
   - Evaluate setting `webPreferences.scrollBounce: true` on macOS only. Electron documents this as macOS scroll rubber-banding support, but it should be tested because it may affect scroll feel throughout the app.
   - Keep this separate from the gesture-to-navigation logic so it can be reverted independently.

5. Testing:
   - Unit test gesture classification with synthetic `WheelEvent`-like inputs: direction, threshold, diagonal rejection, cooldown, and delta-mode normalization.
   - Unit/component test the hook gates behavior to macOS and respects navigation-disabled states.
   - Add a focused test around back/forward route behavior if `navigateForwardFromHistory` introduces explicit forward-state tracking.

## Verification

- Manual QA on a Mac with a trackpad:
  - Navigate from inbox to repository issues to an issue detail; swipe back returns to the list; swipe forward returns to the detail.
  - Swipe back from a detail route with no browser history falls back consistently with the existing parent-route behavior.
  - Vertical scrolling in issue lists, chat, settings, and history panels does not navigate.
  - Horizontal scrolling controls, if present, can still scroll without triggering navigation until the scrollable edge behavior is intentionally accepted.
  - Create issue flow/open forms do not navigate from accidental swipes.
  - Windows/Linux builds show no behavior change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- macOS users can use two-finger horizontal trackpad swipes to navigate backward and forward in the desktop app.
- The implementation is gated to desktop macOS.
- Navigation behavior matches existing route semantics and does not bypass repository/workspace validation.
- Gesture handling is thresholded, debounced, and covered by tests.
- No regressions to existing keyboard shortcuts or scroll behavior.

## References

- Electron BrowserWindow `webPreferences.scrollBounce` macOS option: https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/browser-window
- Electron webContents input events for possible native fallback investigation: https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/web-contents

frontmatter.title: Technical Spec: Support macOS trackpad swipe navigation in desktop

value.title: Technical Spec: Support macOS trackpad swipe navigation in desktop
